title: 10 best herbal creams for difficulty chewing

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to chew.
Licoricidin: This compound has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections in the mouth and throat that may be causing difficulty chewing.
Glycosides: These compounds have soothing and protective effects on the mucous membranes of the mouth and throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort that can make it hard to chew.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with difficulty chewing because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation in the mouth and throat, making it easier to chew and swallow food. Additionally, licorice creams can help to relax the jaw and facial muscles, further alleviating discomfort and pain associated with difficulty chewing. This can lead to improved oral function and overall quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with difficulty chewing, promoting overall oral health and comfort.

Flavonoids (specifically, glycyrrhizin and licoflavonoid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and protect the mouth and throat, making it easier to chew and swallow.

Terpenoids (specifically, 18α-glycyrrhetinic acid): They exhibit anti-inflammatory and anti-irritant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the jaw and mouth, making it easier to chew and eat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are commonly used to make creams for difficulty chewing because of their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe sore throats and promote healing.

Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves are also used to make creams for difficulty chewing due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and throat.

Stems: Glycyrrhiza glabra stems are sometimes used to make creams for difficulty chewing because of their ability to help soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the mouth and throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier to ensure quality.
Combine 50g of the harvested roots with 500ml of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 hours or overnight at a temperature range of 60-80°c.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the infused oil.
Whip 200g of beeswax with 200ml of the infused oil using an electric mixer until smooth.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage creams can reduce inflammation in the jaw, cheeks, and tongue, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing, allowing for smoother and more efficient mastication.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress and damage to the oral mucosa, promoting a healthy environment that supports normal chewing function and reduces the risk of gum disease, tooth decay, and other oral health issues.
Muscle relaxant properties: Sage contains compounds like camphor and thujone, which can help relax tense jaw muscles, reduce spasms, and ease facial tension, making it easier to chew and swallow.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with difficulty chewing because of its anti-inflammatory properties and antioxidant compounds, which help to soothe and calm the muscles in the mouth and throat. The cream's ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation can make eating and speaking more comfortable for individuals with conditions such as dry mouth, temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, or fibromyalgia. By providing relief from pain and discomfort, Salvia officinalis cream can make eating and speaking easier.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to chew.
Thymol: A phenolic monoterpene, thymol has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infections and inflammation in the mouth, promoting a healthy environment for chewing.

Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene, carnosic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the mouth and jaw from oxidative stress and inflammation, making it easier to chew and swallow.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe mouth and throat irritations.
Flowers: Used for their soothing and calming effects, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with difficulty chewing.
Stems: Used due to their antioxidant properties, which can help protect the mouth and throat from damage caused by inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of salvia officinalis leaves when they are in full bloom and dry them for 2-3 weeks.
Combine 250g of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 500ml of vegetable oil in a large saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Whip 200g of beeswax with 100g of coconut oil and 100g of the infused oil mixture to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to numb the mouth and reduce discomfort, making it easier to chew and swallow.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol's decongestant properties help to relieve swollen gums and reduce pain caused by dental issues or inflammation, allowing for smoother chewing and eating.

Volatile oils (including menthone and limonene): The volatile oils in peppermint cream have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to combat oral infections that may be causing difficulty chewing, such as thrush or gingivitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with difficulty chewing because of its cooling and refreshing properties. The menthol content in peppermint creams can help numb the mouth and throat, reducing inflammation and pain associated with conditions such as temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ) or oral surgery. This numbing effect can also help relax the facial muscles, making it easier to chew and swallow food. As a result, peppermint creams can provide temporary relief and comfort for individuals experiencing difficulty chewing.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Acts as a local anesthetic, numbing the pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing, thereby reducing the sensation of pain and improving chewing ease.
Limonene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the mouth and jaw, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing.

Rosmarinic acid: Demonstrates antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the oral tissues from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation, thereby promoting healing and improving chewing function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part of peppermint for oral care due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to numb the mouth and reduce pain.
Leaves: Leaves are also used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth.

Leaves: Additionally, peppermint leaves have antibacterial properties that can help to prevent infection and promote healing in the mouth, making them a valuable addition to creams for difficulty chewing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried mentha x piperita leaves, 10 grams of beeswax, 5 grams of cocoa butter, 2 grams of coconut oil, 2 grams of vitamin e oil, and 1 gram of phenonip preservative.
Melt 10 grams of beeswax, 5 grams of cocoa butter, and 2 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of dried mentha x piperita leaves with 10 grams of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth to extract the herbal liquid.
Mix the melted base with the herbal liquid and add 1 gram of phenonip preservative, 2 grams of vitamin e oil, and stir until well combined.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas like turmeric, ginger, and peppermint contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to chew.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in herbal teas like green tea and chamomile can help protect the mucous membranes in the mouth from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing.

Soothing and calming properties: Herbal teas like lavender and valerian root have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety related to dental issues or other conditions that make chewing difficult.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with difficulty chewing because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the mouth and jaw areas. The camellia sinensis extract in these creams reduces inflammation and swelling, making it easier to chew and swallow. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of the cream help to keep the mouth and jaw lubricated, reducing friction and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing. This makes Camellia sinensis creams a natural and effective solution for those experiencing oral discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, a type of polyphenol, may help with difficulty chewing by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.

Theaflavins: Theaflavins, formed during the fermentation of Camellia sinensis, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help soothe and relax the muscles involved in chewing, thereby reducing pain and discomfort.

Epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG): EGCG, a catechin found in Camellia sinensis, may help with difficulty chewing by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to inflammation and pain in the mouth and jaw, thereby facilitating easier and more comfortable chewing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect oral tissues.
Buds: Utilized for their potential to reduce inflammation and improve mouth health, making them a popular choice for oral care products.
Seeds: Employed for their purported ability to improve oral health and reduce inflammation, although more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of camellia sinensis leaves from the garden and dry them in the shade for 2 hours.
Steep 200g of the dried camellia sinensis leaves in 1l of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Mix 1/2 cup of the cooled camellia sinensis infusion with 1/2 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of vegetable glycerin.
Combine the camellia sinensis mixture with 1/2 cup of aloe vera gel and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly for 10 minutes then remove from heat and let cool before whipping into a cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to chew and swallow.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in aloe vera cream neutralize free radicals that can cause tissue damage and inflammation, promoting overall oral health and comfort while chewing.

Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's gel has been shown to accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue structure, and reducing scarring, which can be particularly beneficial for mouth sores or injuries that may be contributing to difficulty chewing.

remedy_constituents: Aloin: This anthraquinone glycoside helps with difficulty chewing by reducing inflammation and pain in the mouth and gums, making it easier to chew and swallow.
Glucomannan: This polysaccharide helps with difficulty chewing by providing soothing and protective properties to the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel inside Aloe vera leaves is used to soothe and heal mouth sores, inflammation, and wounds, making it easier to chew and swallow.
Gel from Leaves: The inner gel of Aloe vera leaves is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling in the mouth and gums, making chewing easier.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of aloe vera gel and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture at 160f for 10 minutes or until the beeswax fully melts and blends.
Add 10g of shea butter and 5g of coconut oil and stir until the mixture is smooth and silky.
Add 2g of vitamin e oil and 2g of honey and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and thicken for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Echinacea, the primary active compound in Kansas coneflower, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, which can cause difficulty chewing.

remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root and 5 grams of beeswax for herbal cream.
Combine 20 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root and 250 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan.
Simmer the mixture at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes or until the liquid reduces by half.
Mix 5 grams of beeswax with 10 milliliters of jojoba oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Blend the cooled echinacea angustifolia mixture with the beeswax and jojoba oil mixture to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth, jaw, and surrounding areas, making it easier to chew and swallow.

Bornyl acetate is not a major constituent of rosemary, but Bornyl acetate is replaced by borneol: Borneol is a terpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and pain in the mouth, making it easier to chew food.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in antioxidants and essential oils, which help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles involved in chewing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves from mature plants on a sunny day.
Dry the rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 1 hour.
Combine 1/4 cup of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil mixture in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of glycerin to create a smooth cream texture.

remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves by cutting them at the base of the stem using sharp scissors.
Steam distill 250 grams of melissa leaves for 2 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax and 200 grams of jojoba oil in a double boiler to melt.
Add 10 milliliters of the melissa essential oil to the cooled beeswax and jojoba mixture.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and allow it to set for 30 minutes.

This can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing, making it easier to eat and maintain a healthy diet. remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves in large quantities from mature plants grown under organic conditions.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 30 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Infuse 20 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves in 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 6 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth to separate the oil from the plant material and discard the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with the infused oil in a double boiler and heat until the beeswax is fully dissolved.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 handfuls of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers from the garden at dawn.
Chop the harvested foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the chopped foeniculum vulgare with 1 cup of sweet almond oil in a pan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to release the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and store it in the fridge.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram creams helps with difficulty chewing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the gums and mouth tissues. Marjoram's warming effect also relaxes tense muscles and joints, making it easier to open the jaw and move the tongue. Additionally, marjoram's soothing and calming effects can alleviate discomfort and pain associated with dental work or gum disease, allowing for a more comfortable chewing experience.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh origanum majorana leaves and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Combine the origanum majorana leaves with 1 cup of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and steep for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids and let it cool.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax pellets with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a separate saucepan and melt over low heat.
Combine the cooled origanum majorana infused oil with the melted wax and oil mixture and stir until well combined.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with difficulty chewing because of its calming and soothing properties. The anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth, making it easier to chew and swallow. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects can help soothe dry mouth and irritated gums, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable chewing experience. This natural remedy provides relief from common issues like dental work, gum disease, or even stress-induced jaw tension.

Antispasmodic properties: Chamomile's antispasmodic properties can help relax muscle spasms in the jaw and facial areas, reducing tension and stiffness that may be contributing to difficulty chewing.

Sedative properties: Chamomile's sedative properties can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which is often a trigger for bruxism (teeth grinding) and TMJ disorders, common causes of difficulty chewing.

remedy_recipe: Collect high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them thoroughly in a warm oven for 30 minutes.
Grind the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine 20 grams of the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 100 grams of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax has melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping it with an electric mixer until it forms a smooth, creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with difficulty chewing because they contain boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the joints and muscles. This can provide relief for individuals who experience stiffness or pain in the jaw, temporomandibular joint (TMJ), or other areas of the mouth and face, making it easier to chew and eat.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense cream reduces inflammation, which can cause pain and stiffness in the jaw muscles and joints, making it easier to chew and swallow.

Antioxidant properties: Frankincense cream's antioxidants help protect the mouth and throat from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissues and reducing discomfort associated with difficulty chewing.
Analgesic properties: The analgesic compounds in frankincense cream can help numb or reduce pain in the jaw, teeth, and gums, making it more comfortable to chew and eat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, frankincense roots help reduce pain and swelling in the gums and jaw, making it easier to chew.
Barks: The barks of frankincense contain bioactive compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate discomfort and difficulty chewing.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 tablespoons of organic coconut oil, 2 teaspoons of beeswax, and 10 grams of dried boswellia serrata extract in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted.
Add 2 drops of vitamin e oil and 1 drop of tea tree essential oil to the mixture, stirring well to combine.
Continue heating the mixture for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until it reaches a temperature of 120°f to 140°f, then remove from heat.
Pour the hot cream mixture into glass jars or containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature over the next 30-40 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with difficulty chewing because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root cause of the issue. Inflammation in the joints and muscles can lead to stiffness and pain, making it challenging to chew and swallow food comfortably. By applying turmeric cream topically to the affected area, the anti-inflammatory properties are absorbed into the skin and tissues, reducing inflammation and relieving discomfort, allowing for smoother chewing and a more enjoyable mealtime experience.

Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding any solids for 20 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for difficulty chewing are topical remedies designed to alleviate discomfort and pain associated with chewing. They help by reducing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to eat and chew food. The best herbal creams for difficulty chewing include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Echinacea purpurea, Aloe barbadensis, Achillea millefolium, Salvia officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Cinnamomum verum, and Calendula officinalis. If left untreated, difficulty chewing can lead to malnutrition, social isolation, and a poor quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to difficulty chewing that are treated with herbal creams are various skin conditions, such as eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis. These creams often contain natural ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile, which soothe and calm the skin. They may also be used to treat mouth sores, gum inflammation, and dry mouth, making them beneficial for overall oral health and comfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with difficulty chewing is a blend of peppermint, aloe vera, and calendula. Peppermint oil helps to relax the jaw muscles and reduce inflammation, while aloe vera soothes and calms the affected area. Calendula, with its anti-inflammatory properties, promotes healing and tissue repair. Together, these herbal creams create a synergistic effect that can provide relief from difficulty chewing and promote overall oral health. Regular application can lead to improved chewing ability and reduced discomfort.

By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help alleviate discomfort and difficulty chewing, allowing individuals to resume normal eating habits.

Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a clean container.
Mix 200g of a base cream with 10g of the extracted liquid and 5g of a preservative in a stainless steel bowl.
Whip the mixture using a hand mixer for 10 minutes until it thickens and forms a smooth consistency.

Dry the harvested calendula flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Make a calendula infusion by steeping 2 tablespoons of dried flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Combine the calendula infusion with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil to create a smooth mixture.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.
